description: A designed graphic styled as a news article screenshot/card (Bloomberg-style layout) on a white background. At the top, a small red bold all-caps kicker reads 'DEEP DIVE.' Below it, a large black sans-serif headline reads: 'Labor Department Threatens H-1B Ban for Rule-Breaking Businesses.' A timestamp 'Sept. 25, 2025, 11:42 AM EDT' follows, then a byline row with a small circular author headshot, the name 'Parker Purifoy' and title 'Reporter,' plus envelope (email) and X share icons. Beneath is a color photograph of the Frances Perkins Department of Labor Building under a blue sky, with a large navy banner of President Trump bearing an 'AMERICA 250' mark and the words 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST' (the word 'FIRST' partly cropped at the bottom edge) hung beside a giant American flag. The card repackages a news article to promote the administration's threat to bar H-1B visas for non-compliant employers. Propaganda technique: appeal to authority (citing a news outlet) plus reinforcement of the 'American Workers First' branding. Target audience is supporters of restrictive immigration/labor policy who favor prioritizing American workers over foreign-visa hires.
notable_text: DEEP DIVE — Labor Department Threatens H-1B Ban for Rule-Breaking Businesses. (Banner: AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST / AMERICA 250.)
